sections.paragraph:
γαστήρ, τρός, ἡ (Hom.+) gener. inner regions of the body, with its various parts, such as stomach and womb

① belly

ⓐ of the body’s inner regions, but w. ref. to moral obligation τὰ ταμιεῖα τῆς γαστρός innermost chambers of the being (so rendered in OT, An American Translation: Pr 20:27) 1 Cl 21:2 (Pr 20:27).

ⓑ metaph., of pers. defined by primary interest glutton (Hes., Theog. 26 et al.) γαστέρες ἀργαί (ἀργός 2) Tit 1:12.

② womb ἡ γαστὴρ αὐτῆς ὀγκοῦτο GJs 12:3; συλλαμβάνειν ἐν γαστρί (Gen 25:21 LXX Sixtina; Demetr.: 722 fgm 1, 4 Jac.) Lk 1:31; ἐν γαστρὶ λαμβάνειν GJs 4:2, 4 (LXX; En 7:2; ApcMos 1). ἐν γαστρὶ ἔχειν be pregnant (Hdt. 3, 32 et al.; med. wr. since Hippocr. [Hobart 92]; Paus. 4, 33, 3; Artem. 2, 18; 3, 32, 4 et al.; PCairZen 328, 20 and PEnteux 71, 6; PMagd 4, 6 [the three III b.c.]; PFlor 130, 3; LXX; En 99:5) Mt 1:18, 23 (cp. Is 7:14); 24:19; Mk 13:17; Lk 21:23; 1 Th 5:3; Rv 12:2. ἐν τῇ γαστρὶ (εἶναι) B 13:2 (Gen 25:23); GJs 13:3.—B. 253. DELG. M-M. Spicq.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

ref. ref. = reference(s)

OT OT = Old Testament

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

metaph. metaph. = metaphor(ically)

pers. pers. = person(s)

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

En En = I Enoch—List 2

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

med. med. = medicus (medical writer), medical

  1. wr. = writers, writings

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Hobart Hobart = WH., Medical Language of St. Luke—List 6

Paus Paus , perieg., II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

PEnteux PEnteux = Papyrus Enteuxeis—List 4

PMagd PMagd = Papyrus de Magdôla—List 4

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 190
